title: Get proper exit code when running or starting a container.
body:
When we finish running a container, we need to call wait in order
to get the exit code from the container.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
For reference, I think exit codes with `podman run --rm` will still be broken for remote, and I'm not sure exactly how to fix them.

@mheon This seems to work.
```
sh-5.0# podman rm foo
Error: no container with name or ID foo found: no such container
sh-5.0# echo $?
1
sh-5.0# ./bin/podman-remote rm foo
Error: unable to find container "foo": no such container
sh-5.0# echo $?
1
sh-5.0# ./bin/podman-remote rm foo
Error: unable to find container "foo": no such container
sh-5.0# echo $?
1
sh-5.0# ./bin/podman-remote rmi foo
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* unable to find a name and tag match for foo in repotags: no such image
sh-5.0# echo $?
1
```

```
sh-5.0# podman rm d05d4b492e7d81c54f7e395e1d293c7dc6045213faafcf34a2be2a3172af503d
Error: cannot remove container d05d4b492e7d81c54f7e395e1d293c7dc6045213faafcf34a2be2a3172af503d as it is running - running or paused containers cannot be removed without force: container state improper
sh-5.0# echo $?
2
sh-5.0# ./bin/podman-remote rm d05d4b492e7d81c54f7e395e1d293c7dc6045213faafcf34a2be2a3172af503d
Error: cannot remove container d05d4b492e7d81c54f7e395e1d293c7dc6045213faafcf34a2be2a3172af503d as it is running - running or paused containers cannot be removed without force: container state improper
sh-5.0# echo $?
2
```
@rhatdan I expect `podman rm` works properly, but `podman run --rm` likely does not.
